title: ISE Continuous Compliance Monitoring (CoCM) Grace-Period Tracking
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Posture* category — CoCM codes `15090` (CoCM grace started), `15091` (CoCM grace expired), `15092` (CoCM compliance restored). Field: `endpoint_mac`, `grace_remaining_min`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=15091
| stats count by posture_policy
| where count > 100
description: Detects CoCM grace-period expirations at scale — when many endpoints simultaneously hit grace expiry without remediation, indicating a fleet-wide compliance regression (broken agent, missing patch).
value: CoCM provides a window to remediate. When the grace expires fleet-wide, large numbers of endpoints are about to be quarantined; pre-emptive detection allows the platform team to extend grace temporarily and address root-cause.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Scheduled patch rollout window — expected expirations.
mitreAttack: N/A (policy hygiene — measures Continuous Compliance Monitoring grace-window usage, no adversary technique)

controlTest.positiveScenario: Force grace expiry on >100 lab endpoints simultaneously. UC-17.1.65 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Single grace expiry — does not fire.

grandmaExplanation: If a computer becomes non-compliant, ISE gives it some time to fix itself. If many computers run out of time at the same moment, we ring the bell — often it's a fleet-wide problem, not bad behaviour.
